pages.title: About Lilian - Lilian Greenwood MP
headings: h2: Follow Lilian on social media
pages.text_excerpt: About Lilian - Lilian Greenwood MP Skip to main content Home About Lilian Contact Lilian Apply for PMQs tickets Information for schools Christmas Card Competition 2025 Office Standards Policy Help and support Privacy Notice More Menu About Lilian . Lilian Greenwood was first elected on 6th May 2010 to serve as the Labour Member of Parliament for Nottingham South. She was born and raised in Bolton, Lancashire in 1966, and attended Canon Slade School before studying at St. Catharine’s Colleague, University of Cambridge, in the class of 1984. Before becoming an MP, Lilian worked as a regional official for the trade union, UNISON, representing public service workers. She successfully campaigned on a range of issues such as equality, improving pensions, increasing funding for the health service, and workers’ rights. She has remained committed to these issues while in Parliament, speaking in hundreds of debates and regularly questioning Conservative Ministers and Prime Ministers. Lilian has always kept an office in Nottingham City Centre. Since she was elected, she has visited countless community groups, schools, businesses and organisations across the constituency. If you would like Lilian to visit your group or organisation, you can contact her office on 0115 711 7000 . Lilian and her team have helped thousands of individual constituents with a wide range of issues ranging from problems with benefits, child support payments, housing or immigration, to listening to people’s concerns regarding the Government’s legislation and plans. If you would like assistance from Lilian, please contact her at lilian.greenwood.mp@parliament.uk or call 0115 711 7000. Nottingham South Following the 2023 Boundary Review, Nottingham South now includes the wards of Bilborough, Clifton East, Clifton West, Lenton & Wollaton East, the Meadows, Radford, and Wollaton West. As of the 2024 General Election, Nottingham City Centre is now included in the Nottingham East constituency, and Leen Valley ward is in Nottingham North and Kimberley. Nottingham South includes two universities (The University of Nottingham and Nottingham Trent University – Clifton campus) and the Queen’s Medical Centre hospital. If you would like to check if you are a Nottingham South constituent, enter your postcode at: https://members.parliament.uk/FindYourMP . . Minister for Local Transport Lilian has been passionate about Transport issues since she was first appointed to the Transport Select Committee following her election in 2010. Since then, she has held the roles of Shadow Local Transport Minister, Shadow Rail Minister, Shadow Secretary of State for Transport, and Chair of the Transport Committee. As Transport Committee Chair, Lilian was committed to ensuring that the needs of all transport users were addressed, particularly those whose voices are least heard by decision-makers. Throughout her time in Parliament, Lilian has helped to secure £60 million of funding to refurbish Nottingham’s main train station, as well as successfully campaigning for the widening of the A453 and extensions to Nottingham’s tram network. Following Labour’s victory in the 2024 General Election, Lilian became Minister for the Future of Roads. Lilian remained in this position until August 2025, before becoming Minister for Local Transport. If you would like to get in touch with Lilian about transport issues, please fill out the contact form here: https://www.smartsurvey.co.uk/s/contact-department-for-transport/ . Follow Lilian on social media Further details Promoted by Angharad Roberts on behalf of Lilian Greenwood both at 11 St Peter's Gate, Nottingham, NG1 2JF. ©2026 Copyright Labour All rights reserved. pages.title: Contact Lilian - Lilian Greenwood MP
headings: h3: Get in touch - I'm here to listen
h2: Get in Touch with Lilian
h2: Follow Lilian on social media
pages.text_excerpt: Contact Lilian - Lilian Greenwood MP Skip to main content Home About Lilian Contact Lilian Apply for PMQs tickets Information for schools Christmas Card Competition 2025 Office Standards Policy Help and support Privacy Notice More Menu Contact Lilian Contact Lilian Your local MP for Nottingham South Call : 0115 711 7000 Email : lilian.greenwood.mp@parliament.uk Write : Office of Lilian Greenwood MP, House of Commons, London, SW1A 0AA Facebook : www.facebook.com/liliangreenwood Twitter/X : @LilianGreenwood Instagram: www.instagram.com/liliangreenwoodmp Get in touch - I'm here to listen Please note that Lilian is only allowed to take up casework from her constituents. You can double-check that she is your MP by entering your postcode here: https://members.parliament.uk/ . In your email or voicemail, please ensure you have included your full name, address, and any contact details to ensure we can get in touch with you regarding your case. If you have not included these details, please re-send your email with them. MP Surgeries If you would like a virtual one-to-one surgery meeting via MS Teams or Zoom, or a phone call with Lilian, please get in touch at lilian.greenwood.mp@parliament.uk or call 0115 711 7000. Transport If you are emailing Lilian as the Minister for Local Transport, or for other issues relating to the Department of Transport, please fill out the contact form here: Introduction : Contact Department for Transport (smartsurvey.co.uk) . Visiting Parliament If you’re a constituent and would like to visit the Houses of Parliament, please get in touch at lilian.greenwood.mp@parliament.uk or 0115 711 7000 and we can try our best to organise one for you. Tours are free of charge and open to UK residents. Early booking is recommended if a specific date and time is required, or for larger groups. Parliament’s expert guides accompany visitors on the 75-minute tour. The tour guide will: explain the work of UK Parliament and its relevance today outline how the working spaces on the tour are used set out ways constituents can get involved and engage with the democratic process highlight some of the dramatic history of the Palace of Westminster. The Visitor Tour Route includes the Chambers of both Houses, the State rooms in the House of Lords, Central Lobby, St Stephen’s Hall and Westminster Hall. When the Houses are sitting, the full tour route may not always be available and can be subject to last minute changes or cancellation due to parliamentary business. UK Parliament tours are not available on Thursdays, Saturdays, Sundays and Bank Holidays, or on the day of some ceremonial events. Tours are free for but travel costs are the responsibility of individual constituents, unless you are a school group . PMQs Tickets Prime Minister’s Questions (PMQs) in the Commons Chamber is a ticketed event which takes place every Wednesday at 12 noon. Tickets are free of charge and are available only to UK residents, via their local Member of Parliament. For PMQs, advance booking is essential to be sure of entry. MPs have a limited number of tickets so requests should be made as far in advance as possible. For more information, and to request that Lilian applies for tickets on your behalf, please click here . Get in Touch with Lilian Name (Required) First Last Address (Required) Lilian is only allowed to take up casework from her constituents. We ask for your address and postcode in order to ensure you are a Nottingham South constituent. Postcode (Required) Email (Required) Enter Email Confirm Email Comments (Required) Please let us know how we can assist you. Follow Lilian on social media Further details Promoted by Angharad Roberts on behalf of Lilian Greenwood both at 11 St Peter's Gate, Nottingham, NG1 2JF. ©2026 Copyright Labour All rights reserved. pages.title: Christmas Card Competition 2025 - Lilian Greenwood MP
pages.text_excerpt: Christmas Card Competition 2025 - Lilian Greenwood MP Skip to main content Home About Lilian Contact Lilian Apply for PMQs tickets Information for schools Christmas Card Competition 2025 Office Standards Policy Help and support Privacy Notice More Menu Christmas Card Competition 2025 . . The annual competition to design my official Christmas card has now closed and the winners have been decided! The cards featuring the winning design will be professionally printed and sent out to hundreds of individuals, organisations, and people that I’ve worked with, both in Nottingham and across the country. Three runners up have also been chosen, and their designs will feature on the back of the finished card. There are also prizes for runners up, as well as Special Merit awards and a ‘Best in School’ prize for each school taking part. The theme this year was local transport: cycling, walking, taxis and road safety. If any children struggled with the theme they were able to do a brightly designed Christmas card. . . The winning design has been chosen by a panel of local judges including: Tasha Markham – Operations Manager, E.ON Next; Richard Daniels , Founder, Forever Stars; Adam Hussain , Senior Manager, Technical Training & Development Specialist, E.ON Net Zero Academy; Lisa Brackner , People & Development Director, Bridgeway Consultants Ltd .; and Louise Gilmore – Policy Advocacy Manager, E.ON. I’m pleased to say that our main sponsor this year is EON, along with support from Nottingham Bowl, Nottingham Panthers, Notts County Cricket Club, Nottingham Forest FC, Notts County FC, and Theatre Royal. Sponsors donate the printing and postage of the cards, as well as prizes for the winners. . This year’s 2025 overall winner is Charlie Gu, age 10, from Fernwood Primary School in Wollaton. Overall Winner: Charlie Gu, Age 10, Fernwood Primary School 2025 Runner Up: Beauty Ayotunde, Age 7, Welbeck Primary School 2025 Runner Up: Teddy Simeonov, Age 10, Mellers Primary School 2025 Runner Up: Kieran Shaw, Age 15, Nethergate Academy Department for Transport Ministerial Card Winner: Adam Zeb, Age 7, Fernwood Primary School E.ON Winner: Dara Adeagbo, Age 11, Radford Academy Forever Stars Winner: Evelyn Jackson, Age 10, Greenfields Community School Bridgeway Consulting Winner: Theo Jones, Age 8, South Wilford Primary School . Aisha Khan, Age 10, Radford Academy Tasneem Ibrahim, Age 8, Southwold Primary School Aminda Habbes, Age 10, Mellers Primary School Georgie Froggatt, Age 9, Fernwood Primary School Alfie Jones, Age 10, Greenfields Community School Eden Killeavy, Age 7, Fernwood Primary School Mustafa Moheed, Age 7, Fernwood Primary School Freya Johnson, Age 5, Greenfields Community School Deliah Leach, Age 7, Greenfields Community School Kani Mohamad, Age 10, Southwold Primary School Bartlomiej Kurowski, Age 15, Nethergate Academy Isobel Savage, Age 10, Fernwood Primary School Leena Assad, Age 10, Victoria Primary School Olivia Folourunso, Age 10, Fernwood Primary School Evie Hallam, Age 8, Fernwood Primary School Mighty Ayuk, Mellers Primary School Aida Mensah, Age 8, Milford Academy . Pana Hamakhan, Year 2, Radford Academy Aisling Novak, Age 9, South Wilford Primary School Jamilia Choudhary, Age 7, Edna G Olds Academy Athalita Azzahra, Age 10, Southwold Primary School Daisy Nelson, Age 9, Firbeck Academy Abigail Mcleod, Age 15, Oak Field School Rosa Neary, Year 4, Milford Academy Princewill Nnadi, Age 12, Westbury Academy Lana Khider, Age 10, Victoria Primary School Laelle Kansichi, Age 10 Djanogly Strelley Academy Mateusz Grzywinska, Age 7, Blessed Robert Widmerpool Yaly Siu, Age 10, Portland Spencer Academy Tilly Warren, Age 10, Middleton Primary School Bhavyasri Palla, Age 10, Greenfields Community School . 2024 Overall Winner: Aadvik Ranjan, Age 6, Welbeck Primary School . Last year I also sent a Christmas card in my capacity as the then Minister for the Future of Roads. Ministerial Card Winner: Grace Plant, Age 9, Fernwood Primary School. . 2

pages.title: Help and support - Lilian Greenwood MP
pages.text_excerpt: Help and support - Lilian Greenwood MP Skip to main content Home About Lilian Contact Lilian Apply for PMQs tickets Information for schools Christmas Card Competition 2025 Office Standards Policy Help and support Privacy Notice More Menu Help and support If you are a constituent in Nottingham South, please do contact Lilian at any point if you think she can assist you. Lilian is here to work and represent you and has supported thousands of constituents since she was first elected in 2010. Urgent Assistance In an emergency, please call 999. NHS – 111 Nottinghamshire Police – 101 Nottingham City Council – 0115 915 5555 Nottingham Housing Aid – 0115 876 3300 or housingaid@nottinghamcity.gov.uk Meadows Advice Group – 0115 986 0197 Clifton Advice Centre – 0115 940 5551 Juno Women’s Aid (Domestic Abuse) – 0808 800 0340 Wellness in Mind Nottingham – 0800 561 0073 Childline – 0800 1111 Housing and homelessness Shelter – 0344 515 2000 Nottingham Housing Aid – 0115 876 3300 or housingaid@nottinghamcity.gov.uk Street Support Nottingham – nottingham@streetsupport.net Framework Housing Association – 0115 841 7711 Food banks Please note that this is not an extensive list of food banks in Nottingham. Please visit the Trussell Trust website if you do not see your area below. The Meadows – 0303 040 1110 – Bridgeway Centre, Arkwright Street, The Meadows, Nottingham, NG2 2JD. Clifton – 07538 916 757 – Hope Centre, Southchurch Drive, Clifton, Nottingham, NG11 8AR. Wollaton – 0303 040 1110 – Grangewood Methodist Church, Grangewood Road, Nottingham, NG8 2SJ. Bilborough – 0303 040 1110 – Bilborough Library, Bracebridge Drive, Bilborough, Nottingham, NG8 4PN. Nottingham City Council For help with bins, council tax, adult social care or licences, please use the Nottingham City Council website or call 0115 915 5555. Councillors are elected to the local council to represent a ward and local community. Find your ward and local councillors by clicking here . Legal advice Nottingham Law Centre – 0115 978 7813 – 119 Radford Road, Hyson Green, Nottingham, NG7 5DU. NLS Legal (based at Nottingham Law School) is for those unable to afford legal services and who are unable to access legal aid. You can make an enquiry here . Citizens Advice Nottingham – 0808 278 7974 – 16-18 Maid Marian Way, Nottingham NG1 6HS, United Kingdom. Children and young people If you are concerned about a child in Nottingham you can contact the city’s Multi Agency Safeguarding (Children) Hub (City MASH) at 0115 876 4800 or CityMASH@nottinghamcity.gov.uk. The Hub is open Monday to Friday from 8:30 am to 4:50 pm. Outside of these hours, the telephone number should only be used for emergencies. Base 51 – supports 11-25 year olds – 0115 952 5040 or info@base51.org.uk. Nottingham City Council Youth Service provides information on youth provision, children’s activity centres, activities for children and families, and holiday activities. Support for pensioners I would encourage everyone to check what they are eligible for the following resources: Check if you are eligible for Pension Credit online at www.gov.uk/pension-credit/eligibility or call 0800 99 1234 . ⁠Check if you are eligible for Attendance Allowance, a disability benefit for those over retirement age, which is not means tested, at www.gov.uk/attendance-allowance or call 0800 731 0122 . Contact Nottingham City Council to discuss Housing Benefit and Council Tax Reduction. Check can find further information here: https://www.nottinghamcity.gov.uk/information-for-residents/benefits/ . ⁠Contact your local Citizen’s Advice on 0808 802 2000 . Turn2Us – The charity Turn2Us can help people fill out forms and check eligibility. Visit https://www.turn2us.org.uk or call 0808 802 2000. ⁠Use the Turn2Us online benefits calculator: benefits-calculator.turn2us.org.uk/. Check if you are eligible for any grants with Turn2Us: grants-search.turn2us.org.uk/ . Dementia Support Nottinghamshire Carers Association – 01773 833833. Other Dementia support and
